title: anthurium chiapasense subsp. tlaxiacense
intro: Anthurium chiapasense subsp. tlaxiacense, also known as the tlaxiacense anthurium, is a rare, flowering plant species belonging to the Arum family (Araceae) native to Mexico. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to the presence of bioactive compounds such as alkaloids and glycosides. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, making it a potential remedy for various ailments. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, infusion, or ointment by the local communities, who also use it in folk medicine to promote healing and well-being.
